reviews.text: I stumbled upon functional medicine through an extended family member and met with Jeff several years ago. I had no idea how unhealthy my body was at the time; all I know is I was miserable and knew something was terribly wrong. Jeff walked me through my health history dating back to my childhood and determined what may have been contributing to my problems. We adjusted supplements and focused on getting my gut health back to normal. Jeff essentially saved my life as he changed everything for me in terms of my overall health. I will also be grateful for all the time he took with me, showing patience and grace and dedication to being a true healer.

reviews.text: Under Jeff Evilsizor's care, I have fully recovered from multiple chronic conditions and gut-related issues, with non-invasive treatment. Jeff is an incredibly kind and compassionate practitioner, as well as being the most knowledgable and helpful medical professional I have ever worked with. Jeff will listen intently and truly partner with you in your healing process -- to find and fix the underlying issue, not cover up symptoms. He considers and treats the whole person, with a deep understanding of the interconnectedness of the mind and body, and the influence of gut health on all systems in the body. If you suffer from any kind of chronic health condition, gut, autoimmune, or otherwise I highly recommend making an appointment with Jeff Evilsizor at Vivo Integrative Medicine.

reviews.text: Jeff has been so caring and supportive in working with me and figuring out how to treat my hashimotos. He truly listens and cares about finding the best options for his patients. reviews.name: places/ChIJS8l0nWvbwIcRfaITTGp7Tjk/reviews/ChdDSUhNMG9nS0VJQ0FnSUNKaWFITWdnRRAB
reviews.text:
Jeff entered into my complicated medical journey a number of years ago at a time when I was going through so many things, with health, family, work, etc.  He has never rushed me through an appointment or seemed distracted when discussing my journey in totality.  He is interested in the whole picture/person and that interest and concern has helped tremendously as I have navigated through life’s challenges in all areas. reviews.name: places/ChIJYSPGZisr34cRVTaaFnWDJKI/reviews/Ci9DQUlRQUNvZENodHljRjlvT2tKSFdGOVphVEI0ZUUwMFpFSlZURXhYWHpkMU1VRRAB
reviews.text:
The young ladies at the front desk have always been great. I’ve been a client of Summit for over 3 years and have generally had a good experience.

Unfortunately, I ran into an issue this weekend that was handled very poorly. On Saturday, my wife accidentally drove off with my prescription sitting on top of the car. I had just picked it up on Friday, cleaned my car, set the prescription on top while grabbing the kids, and it was gone by the time we realized.

When I called the office to explain what happened, I was told there was nothing they could do and that I would have to wait another 30 days to receive my order. No options and no flexibility.

This is especially frustrating considering Summit offers services like testosterone therapy, peptides, and other treatments specifically designed to help clients feel better and function at their best—yet they were willing to let a client of over 3 years go an additional 25+ days without treatment.

Out of curiosity, I called two competitors in the area and explained the exact same situation. Both said I could pay a fine and they would replace my supplements.

After being a loyal client for years, the lack of understanding or willingness to work with me was disappointing.

——I appreciate you getting back to me. Just to clarify, I did misplace one week’s worth on a work trip, but never a full month. I’ve picked up early a couple of times—just a few days—because your office is closed most of the week and I happened to be in the area when it worked for my schedule. That felt different than asking for extra doses.

I understand that this situation ultimately stems from my mistake, and I take responsibility for that. At the same time, I feel that clearer communication and a defined protocol could have simplified this situation significantly. If there were a clear policy—such as a once-per-year courtesy refill for accidents—and that was communicated upfront, it wouldn’t feel like decisions are being made on the fly.

If I had known that in advance, when I misplaced a week’s worth, I would have gone without it and reserved that option for a situation like this—where I’m now without a full prescription for over 25 days.—

reviews.name: places/ChIJYSPGZisr34cRVTaaFnWDJKI/reviews/ChdDSUhNMG9nS0VJbjBfYl83aTR5aS1RRRAB
reviews.text:
5 Stars – Truly Life-Changing Experience!

I can’t say enough great things about this Summit Rejuvination! From our first visit I was immediately impressed by the professionalism, warmth, and care from the entire staff. Everyone from the front desk to the medical team made me feel welcome, respected, and genuinely cared for!

What really sets this clinic apart is their extensive knowledge and consultative approach. reviews.text:
DO NOT GO HERE FOR HELP with your hormones they will steal your money, lie and leave you with no help at all.   I went to there so called free consultation. I told them I had night sweats. Facial hair, low energy. No libido.  Hair loss. Stubborn weight. I even have hormonal rosacea. All the classic pre menopause issues. Im 42. This is just to name a few. They tell me they can test me and give me my first treatment for just over $700. I tell them im taking wild yam cream to help with my hormone issues and wanted to know if I should come back after I let it leave my system for accurate results. They lied to me and told me it wont effect my results as long as they draw blood from the opposite arm i rubbed the hormone cream on. I now know it does effect results. They lied to me. I waited for my results. Weeks!!! They tell me all my labs came back normal and nothing was wrong with my hormones. But they weren't my FSH was lower then a girl who has never had a period. My T3 and T4 were very low. My estrogen was boarderline.  I said I cant have all the issues im dealing with and have balanced hormones. They didnt care. They had no recommendations for me. I said I wanted to speak to a doctor.  They refused. Didnt refund me any of the money i paid. I told them. You guys told me the fee covered the test. My first treatment and a visit with the doctor.  Since i never got treatment nor did i see a doctor i should get some of my money back.  They proceded to lie about how the $700+ only covers the test. They sold me on the whole package and now when i dont get it and want some pf my money back they lied and then ignored my request to speak to the doctor.  They are not in the business to treat hormone issues. They are in the businesses of getting you in the door to charge you an insane amount of money for testing. If you go to quest diagnostic and pay for your own labs its like $112 bucks. I went to my endocrinologist afterwards and he told me you cant look at labs and say a women is normal because you must go off symtoms as well. He explained what is normal for one women maybe abnormal for another. He said you need to listen to each women and look at her labs and make choices based off that to help her feel like herself again. Some women need more testosterone, while others need more estrogen or more progesterone. No 2 women are the same. reviews.name: places/ChIJhdTQAQT3wIcRso1RCO-jF6c/reviews/Ci9DQUlRQUNvZENodHljRjlvT2s1dGFVVllWMHRtV2s1b2FFbDVSWEoyV1hCSGFsRRAB
reviews.text:
I was there on the 18th for a initial consultation . Saw NP Evans. At least  I am assuming she was NP.   She would not let me talk and explain to her at first a complication that I had that would probably prevent what I was there for (and it did) and further explain the places I had already been (why I left them), the conditions ED I was already being treated for and the main reason I was there to see if she could take over a Tri-Mix RX I had.

I was exhausted but did not want to move the appointment a second time out of courtesy.

Its a VERY big red flag to me when the provider I am seeing does NOT listen to the patient.  Yes I know she needed some medication dose clarifications but most of what she needed I would have told her, AND it was critical information, AND IT could have waited five minutes on her part for me to let me speak.  She simply seemed to not care if anything I said might have had value.  Not acceptable.

If your patient (or potential customer in this case and patient) tells you they have something important to say YOU be quiet  listen. Former Paramedic here.  They are your very best source of information.

I do not really blame her for not helping me as it was a condition outside of her control that stopped it for  the primary reason I booked the appointment (P Shot) and its her call on the RX but I disagree with it.

She went over the pricing sheet with me at the end and I got a copy.  This place DOES NOT take insurance except for your lab work. Not sure about CareCredit  Its $250 for new patient work up and labs. One time fee.  Paid to the NP and Clinic she told me.

But what bothers me is that even though your an established patient its $65 for every follow up visit. Medication Follow Up. $100 bucks.  I have to wonder what ELSE they are going to charge item per item, you, that are not listed.  God knows what a P Shot or Something Called Shockwave Therapy would cost you.  I am betting TONS of money.   I know they are very very expensive.

My main concern (and a pretty valid one) is that if a provider will not listen to me the,  patient initially , then HOW could I expect her or KC Wellness to listen to me when I tell them something when I have thousands of dollars invested in the  treatment P. Shot Shockwave, whatever. down the road.

I am sorry but I left feeling (after the above)  that I am probably going to be very relucent in 5 or 6 moths moving forward with then and investing the kind of money we are looking at, to try and treat my condition, and that I would be nickel and dimed for everything.

I am not recommending TO GO or NOT TO GO to KC Wellness for your treatment needs.  Most seem happy with them.   That is UP to you.  I just felt my experience need to be shared after thinking on it for a day or so.

Be Safe and God Bless.

reviews.text: I can not recommend Sandi highly enough!  I've been seeing her for 15 to 20 years now for bio-identical hormone replacement. Sandi takes the time needed whenever I have an appointment and has accommodated my crazy work and travel schedule whenever I've asked.  If you need a thorough health care person to guide you thru hormonal issues using both prescriptions and supplements, you can find no better person than Sandi!

reviews.text:
Had an appointment with Sandy but ended up in the ER an hour before the appointment. Called her to let her know. I have never missed an appointment. I have left two text an a phone message. No reply to any of them. Very frustrating after spending a lot of money with her.

I am happy to post all the text to your office. Unfortunately I do not have the phone call. My first text to you was September 10th. Phone call on October 8th. Another text on November 20th and again on November 22nd. Finally 2 hour after this review I get a response.
Reviews and responses should always be honest. Very disappointed in the lack on honesty from you.

reviews.text: Wittmer is my second clinic.  My first clinic was great but had limited Hours. This is not a problem at Wittmer. I’m telling you, Wittmer has taken it to the next level. They have blown me away with the level of customer service they provide. The communication is above what you could expect as thy are very responsive.  Their appointments are super easy and do not require in person visits.  I simple go have labs locally drawn when required and everything else is handled via video or phone call. Their prescribed treatment plan arrives through the mail and all I have to do is follow the prescribers directions.  It could not be easier.  I am seeing the desired results as promised.  Their treatment options are many and thy customize to your needs and goals.  They have also recently started doing First Responder/Military discounts that are lifetime once verified. I would highly recommend them to anyone interested in taking their health goals to the next level.

reviews.text: I started my journey two months ago… before finding Wittmer Rejuvenation Clinic I was simply not myself… not even close. I was so tired and all the time. I started to think I was dying. reviews.text:
Since the start of the year my testosterone levels reached 265 for a man who was about to turn 40 this year. I felt like crap and mind always seemed clouded. I exercised regularly, but motivation seemed harder to maintain and recovery times began to get longer after each workout.

I asked my primary care dr and I was still not at levels that insurance would cover for cost of trt. I just got tired of it and found renew and they helped me on a treatment plan. Granted it's not cheap since insurance will not cover cost, but I consider this a investment into my health and well being. It has been 6 months+ since I started and man do I not regret it.

My mood has improved a ton, my energy is much higher, and the added bonus of a better sex drive for my wife. I am now at normal range for testosterone around 800ish and the difference from mid 200 to that is night and day. When taking this make sure you exercise regularly and constantly. No point in fixing as well as paying to balance your testosterone if you're not going to use it to the highest potential.

Jason from renew if you're reading this brother much appreciated for the info and help on this journey. I just feel over all much better and the best way I can put it is like I am in my early 20s again. To the men who feel that weight of father time on your body try it. Having low testosterone as a man can ruin the quality of life for a man and I know the feeling of being a shell of your former self.

Now this will not make you superman, but it can help you feel what you used to be all those years ago. Just remember if you do this do it all the way. Exercise regularly, eat for the most part healthy, and most of all remember how it felt to you know enjoy life. Best wishes to those who begin.

reviews.text:
Just here to post an annual review regarding this company and their scummy ownership. My experience was at their Chesterfield location, but they are all owned by the same owner.

This guy, quite literally, charged me $2200 for services not rendered/product not delivered. I provided all proof of it to him. He claims I signed up for an "auto-pay" feature, and yet even on the paperwork he provided you will find that no AutoPay option/frequency was selected. He still refused to refund me.

There are a million different clinics out there that offer these services; find a different one.

Look at their one star reviews and you will find they've done similar things to MANY people.

They also had a massive issue where they would tell individuals that their insurance provider would cover their blood work. When the insurance company refused, the customer would be stuck with bills for thousands of dollars. I only know this as a good friend of mine was stuck with a massive bill -- and once again -- if you read the reviews -- you will find it has happened to many others.

I cannot tell you enough to avoid this company at all costs.

reviews.text: BEWARE OF SUMMIT. Because of Summit, I was recently sent to a medical billing collection agency for an unjustified $1,800 debt. Summit violated federal law by neglecting to inform me of the No Surprise Act law which requires providers like Summit to give me an estimate of health services that are out-of-network with my health insurance. If I had known before my blood draw done at Summit that the blood tests that Summit offered would cost me $1,800, then I would never have pursued them. Summit marketed that the blood tests would cost only $100. I am currently filing both federal and Missouri complaints through the attorney general's office against Summit for violating the No Surprise Act law.
